title: Albite and/or anorthite glass ceramic material exhibing thermal warmth
abstract: The present invention relates to an albite and/or anorthite glass ceramic material, which exhibits a sensation of thermal warmth and its production process. Furthermore, the present invention relates to the use of this glass-ceramic material for the manufacturing of industrial, structural and ornamental ceramic products.
claims:
Claims 1 . Glass-ceramic material which exhibits a sensation of thermal warmth, characterized in that it has a thermal conductivity between 0.25 to 0.5 Wm "1 K "1 and comprising 芒聙垄 albite NaAISisOs microcrystals and nanocrystals , 芒聙垄 optionally, anorthite CaAI 2 Si 2 Os microcrystals and nanocrystals 芒聙垄 and vitreous phase, wherein the microcrystals are separated by a region formed by the nanocrystals and the vitreous phase, and wherein said the vitreous phase is in a proportion less than 18 % with respect to the total weight of the glass-ceramic material. 2. Glass-ceramic material according to the preceding claim, comprising anorthite CaAI 2 Si 2 Os microcrystals and nanocrystals. 3. Glass-ceramic material according to any of claims 1 or 2, wherein the albite microcrystals have elongated morphologies with smaller diameter of between 0.3 脦录脦鹿脧聞脦鹿 and 1 .5 脦录脦鹿脧聞脦鹿 and larger diameter of between 1 .5 脦录脦鹿脧聞脦鹿 and 8 脦录脦鹿脧聞脦鹿. 4. Glass-ceramic material, according to any of claims 1 to 3, wherein the anorthite microcrystals have elongated morphologies with smaller diameter of between 0.2 脦录脦鹿脧聞脦鹿 and 0.5 脦录脦鹿脧聞脦鹿 and larger diameter of between 5 脦录脦鹿脧聞脦鹿 and 30 脦录脦鹿脧聞脦鹿. 5. Glass-ceramic material, according to any of claims 1 to 4, wherein said material contains at least a weight percent with respect to the end material expressed in terms of the following equivalent oxides: 芒聙垄 between 45 % and 68 % of SiO 2 , 芒聙垄 between 15 % and 27 % of AI 2 O 3 , 脗路 between 4.5 % and 13 % of CaO, 芒聙垄 between 0 % and 9 % of SrO, 芒聙垄 between 1 % and 5. 5 % of Na 2 O, 

 芒聙垄 between 1 % and 5 % of K 2 O, 芒聙垄 between 0 % and 2% of B 2 O 3 , 芒聙垄 between 0 % and 14 % of ZrO 2 , 芒聙垄 and between 0% and 5 % of MgO, BaO and ZnO. 6. Glass-ceramic material according to the preceding claim, wherein the weight percent with respect to the end material expressed in terms of equivalent oxide of SiO 2 is between 49 % and 60 %. 7. Glass-ceramic material according to claim 5, wherein the weight percent with respect to the end material expressed in terms of equivalent oxide of AI2O3 is between 17 % and 25 %. 8. Glass-ceramic material according to the preceding claim, wherein the weight percent with respect to the end material expressed in terms of equivalent oxide of AI2O3 is between 19 % and 23 %. 9. Glass-ceramic material according to claim 5, wherein the weight percent with respect to the end material expressed in terms of equivalent oxide of CaO is between 6 % and 12 %. 10. Glass-ceramic material according to the preceding claim, wherein the weight percent with respect to the end material expressed in terms of equivalent oxide of CaO is between 7.5 % and 1 1 %. 1 1 . Glass-ceramic material according to claim 5, wherein the weight percent with respect to the end material expressed in terms of equivalent oxide of Na2O is between 1 .5 % and 4 %. 12. Glass-ceramic material according to the preceding claim, wherein the weight percent with respect to the end material expressed in terms of equivalent oxide of Na2O is between 2 % and 3.5 %. 13. Glass-ceramic material according to claim 5, wherein the weight percent with respect to the end material expressed in terms of equivalent oxide of K 2 O is between 1 .5 % and 4 %. 14. Glass-ceramic material according to the preceding claim, wherein the weight percent with respect to the end material expressed in terms of equivalent oxide of K 2 O is between 2 % and 3 %. 15. Glass-ceramic material according to any of claims 1 to 14, wherein 芒聙垄 it further comprises zirconia crystals Zr0 2 or zirconium silicate crystals ZrSiO 4 , where said crystals are distributed throughout the region of nanocrystals and vitreous phase which separate the microcrystals of the glass- ceramic material, and wherein the material displays a thermal conductivity between 0.31 a 0.45 Wm "1 K "1 . 16. Glass-ceramic material according to the preceding claim, wherein the zirconia crystals Zr0 2 may be in the form cubic, tetragonal or monoclinic. 17 Glass-ceramic material according to any of claims 15 or 16, wherein the zirconia crystals Zr0 2 have an average size of between 50 and 200 nm. 18. Glass-ceramic material according to claim 15, wherein the zirconium silicate crystals ZrSiO 4 have an average size of between 200 nm and 500 nm. 19. Glass-ceramic material according to any of claims 15 to 18, wherein the weight percent of the zirconia crystals Zr0 2 or of the zirconium silicate crystals ZrSiO 4 expressed in terms in equivalent oxide with respect to the total weight of the glass-ceramic material is between 2 % and 14 %. 20. Glass-ceramic material according to the preceding claim, wherein the 

 weight percent of the zirconia crystals ZrO2 or of the zirconium silicate crystals ZrSiO 4 expressed in terms in equivalent oxide with respect to the total weight of the glass-ceramic material is between 2 % and 8 %. 21 . Production process of the glass-ceramic material according to any of claims 1 to 20, comprising the following steps: a) melting at least one compound selected from the list comprising sassolite H3BO3, quartz S1O2, dolomite CaMg(CO3)2, calcium carbonate CaCO3, zincite ZnO, kaolin AI 2 Si2O 5 (OH) , strontianite SrCO3, alumina AI2O3, calcium phosphate Ca3(PO 4 )2, a zirconium compound selected from among zirconia ZrO2 or zirconium silicate ZrSiO 4 and an alkali feldspar selected from among albite NaAISisOs and anorthite CaAI 2 Si2O8, or any combination thereof, at a temperature of between 1400 脗掳C and 1550 脗掳C, and fritting; b) grinding the frit obtained in step a) with kaolin; c) forming the product obtained in b); d) sintering the product formed in c) at temperatures of between 980 脗掳C and 1280 脗掳C. 22. Process according to the preceding claim, wherein the percentage of the frit used in the grinding mixture of step b) is between 70 % and 96 wt%. 23. Process according to any of claims 21 or 22, wherein the percentage of kaolin is between 4 % and 15 wt% in step b). 24. Process according to any of claims 21 to 23, wherein step d) of sintering is carried out at temperatures of between 1 100 脗掳C and 1220 脗掳C. 25. Process according to any of claims 21 to 24, comprising an additional step e), after the step of sintering (d), of rectifying and polishing with abrasives. 26. Use of the glass-ceramic material according to any of claims 1 to 20, for the manufacturing of industrial, structural or ornamental ceramic products. 27. Use according to the preceding claim, for the manufacturing of floor tiles or glazes of floor tiles. 28. Use of the glass-ceramic material according to any of claims 1 to 20, as thermal insulator. 29. Use of the glass-ceramic material according to claim 28, as a component of a cool roof, cool room or cool fagade.
